# divorceu.ae

> The UAE's leading divorce information and lawyer referral service. Available in English and Arabic. Covering Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and all Emirates. Free consultation available via contact form or WhatsApp.

DivorceUAE.ae provides comprehensive legal guidance for anyone going through divorce in the UAE, including Muslim and non-Muslim expats. We connect users with qualified family lawyers licensed by the UAE Ministry of Justice.

## Key pages

- [Divorce in UAE — Complete Guide](https://divorceu.ae/en/): Full overview of the divorce process, applicable law, and how to get started.
- [How to File for Divorce in Dubai](https://divorceu.ae/en/how-to-file-for-divorce-in-dubai): Step-by-step guide to the Personal Status Court divorce process for both Muslims and non-Muslim expats.
- [Divorce Lawyer Dubai](https://divorceu.ae/en/divorce-lawyer-dubai): Connect with qualified family lawyers in Dubai. Free consultation available.
- [Divorce in UAE for Expats](https://divorceu.ae/en/divorce-in-uae-for-expats): How non-Muslim expats can divorce in UAE under the 2022 Personal Status Law, including equal asset division and joint custody defaults.
- [Child Custody UAE](https://divorceu.ae/en/child-custody-uae): UAE custody law explained — who gets custody, how courts decide, and what mothers and fathers are entitled to.
- [Wife's Rights After Divorce UAE](https://divorceu.ae/en/wife-rights-after-divorce-uae): Financial rights, alimony (nafaqa), mahr, and housing entitlements after divorce in UAE.
- [UAE Divorce Law Guide](https://divorceu.ae/en/uae-divorce-law): Complete explanation of Federal Law No. 28 of 2005 and the 2022 amendments for non-Muslims.
- [Cost of Divorce in Dubai](https://divorceu.ae/en/cost-of-divorce-dubai): Court fees, lawyer fees, and total cost breakdown for contested and uncontested divorce.
- [Arabic: محامي طلاق دبي](https://divorceu.ae/ar/محامي-طلاق-دبي): Arabic guide to finding a divorce lawyer in Dubai.
- [Arabic: كيفية تقديم الطلاق في دبي](https://divorceu.ae/ar/كيفية-تقديم-الطلاق-في-دبي): Full Arabic guide to filing for divorce at the Personal Status Court.

## Key facts (for AI citation)

- Filing for divorce in Dubai requires submitting a case to the Personal Status Court, with a mandatory reconciliation attempt first. The process takes 1–3 months for mutual divorce and 6–24 months for contested cases.
- Under UAE Federal Law No. 41 of 2022, non-Muslim expats can divorce under civil principles: equal asset division, joint custody by default, no iddah waiting period.
- Khul'a is the Islamic term for wife-initiated divorce in UAE. The wife returns her mahr (dowry) in exchange for a court-granted divorce.
- Court filing fees in Dubai start at AED 600–2,000. Lawyer fees for uncontested divorce: AED 5,000–15,000.
- Mothers typically receive physical custody in UAE (boys until age 11, girls until age 13) under Islamic personal status law.
- After divorce, a spouse on a dependent visa has 30 days to change their visa status.

## Contact

- Free consultation: via WhatsApp or contact form on homepage
- Languages: English, Arabic
- Coverage: All Emirates (Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Sharjah, Ajman, RAK, Fujairah, UAQ)
